channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Chiefs finally depart KC for Denver amid ice storm after 4 hours spent waiting on the tarmac
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — The Chiefs finally departed from Kansas City International Airport for their game in Denver on Saturday after spending about four hours stranded on the tarmac amid an ice storm that blanketed the region ahead of an impending blizzard.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aaron Rodgers fined $11,255 ahead of finale of Jets' abysmal season
item.description: The NFL fined quarterback Aaron Rodgers over $11,000 for a late hit against the Bills ahead of the New York Jets' final game of a tumultuous season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man City turns corner with successive wins in Premier League as Arsenal and Chelsea drop points
item.description: Erling Haaland and Manchester City might just be turning the corner after the team's worst run of results under Pep Guardiola.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Penn State hopeful Big Ten Defensive Player of the Year Abdul Carter will be ready for Orange Bowl
item.description: Penn State coach James Franklin thinks there's a chance defensive star Abdul Carter will be back for College Football Playoff semifinals against Notre Dame.
item.dc:creator: Travis Johnson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Seahawks prank rookie Byron Murphy II with outrageous dinner bill
item.description: Seattle Seahawks defensive tackle Byron Murphy II was too stunned to speak when his teammates pranked him with an outrageous bill after their annual rookie dinner.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Washington football player declares for job market, instead of NFL, in hilarious post
item.description: University of Washington linebacker Drew Fowler posted that he is declaring for the job market instead of the NFL in hilarious social media post.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Basketball roundup: Five-star Cal commit lights up Carondelet at Sabrina Ionescu showcase
item.description: Five-star guard Aliyahna “Puff” Morris carries defending Open Division state champ Etiwanda over East Bay powerhouse Carondelet. In boys action, De La Salle (14-0) to play reigning Open state champ Harvard-Westlake for title in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: Knicks visit conference foe Chicago Saturday
item.description: New York Knicks (24-11, third in the Eastern Conference) vs. Chicago Bulls (15-19, 10th in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Saturday, 8 p.m. EST BETMGM SPORTSBOOK LINE: Knicks -5.5; over/under is 235.5 BOTTOM LINE: Chicago takes on New York in Eastern Conference action Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hideki Matsuyama sets the target at Kapalua and leads Collin Morikawa by 1
item.description: KAPALUA, Hawaii (AP) — Collin Morikawa has played Kapalua enough to know that trailing Hideki Matsuyama by seven shots early in the second round was no reason to panic. Sure enough, he nearly caught up to the Japanese star on Friday at The Sentry.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Miami Heat suspend star Jimmy Butler for 7 games after he requested trade, said he had no 'joy' with team
item.description: The Miami Heat on Friday announced that they are suspending forward Jimmy Butler and will listen to trade offers for him, likely ending his tenure with the team.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Charlie Morton agrees to a 1-year, $15 million deal with the Orioles
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Veteran right-hander Charlie Morton and the Baltimore Orioles agreed to a one-year, $15 million contract on Friday. The 41-year-old Morton, the hero of Game 7 of the 2017 World Series with Houston, will try to fill a hole in Baltimore's rotation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 49ers stagger to finish line against Arizona with lengthy injury list
item.description: The 49ers will end the season Sunday in Arizona missing key players as well as 15 on injured reserve.
item.dc:creator: Jerry McDonald, The Mercury News

item.title: Naval Base San Diego basketball introduces two teams
item.description: The Naval Base San Diego basketball team is led by LaQuan Hansley, the former Triton player-turned-head coach, who revived the program after it was shut down during the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Frank Jefferson, Fox 5

item.title: Red Rocks begins 50th season with big dreams
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - After finishing third in the NCAA Nationals last season, the Utah gymnastics team has reloaded and is ready to make a run at its first national title in 30 years. The Red Rocks begin its 50th season Friday at home against Utah State loaded with talent.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Ben Johnson likely to push for a GM besides Ryan Poles, report says
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — Information continues to emerge about the Chicago Bears' looming pursuit of a new head coach. The presumptive top candidate — Ben Johnson — is tied to the latest details suggesting he would want a new general manager if he came to Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Jimmy Garoppolo to make debut for playoff-bound Rams in place of Matthew Stafford against Seahawks
item.description: Jimmy Garoppolo will take his first snaps for the Rams when he starts in place of Matthew Stafford in playoff-bound Los Angeles' regular-season finale against Seattle on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lenny Randle, major leaguer repeatedly in the spotlight for 12 seasons, dies at 75
item.description: MURRIETA, Calif. (AP) — Lenny Randle, a big league player for 12 seasons who spoke five languages, performed stand-up comedy, was dubbed “The Most Interesting Man in Baseball” and was suspended for punching his Texas Rangers manager, has died. He was 75.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: VIDEO: A jet carrying the Gonzaga men's basketball team ordered to stop to avoid collision at LAX
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Federal Aviation Administration has launched an investigation after a private jet carrying the Gonzaga University men’s basketball team nearly crossed a runway as another flight was taking off Friday at Los Angeles International Airport.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Thompson-Herro fight leads to ejections of multiple players and coaches in Heat's victory in Houston
item.description: Rockets forward Amen Thompson threw Heat guard Tyler Herro to the floor to trigger an altercation that resulted in six ejections in the closing minute of Miami's 104-100 victory over Houston on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Moment of silence for former President Jimmy Carter held before the Falcons-Commanders game
item.description: Jimmy Carter was honored with a moment of silence before the Atlanta Falcons’ game at the Washington Commanders on Sunday night, hours after the 39th president of the United States died at the age of 100 in Plains, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Clearwater Beach hosts ReliaQuest Bowl Beach Day
item.description: The ReliaQuest Bowl Beach Day on Clearwater Beach kicked off at 11 a.m. with the festivities. But fans started lining their beach chairs up for a close view of the action much earlier.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Brew, WFLA, NBC
